rulings.Case Title: LYDIA BACA VS. MALINKA TACUMA MOYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Fabricated $42,620.00 Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT MALINKA MOYE MOTION TO SET ASIDE FABRICATED $42,620.00 DEFAULT JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Any future motions must be properly served on Lydia Baca's attorney of record William Choy at the counsel's address of record =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2023 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT BENITA CHIU DEMURRER TO AMEND CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer to the UCL cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant fails to allege a business practice that caused loss of money or property giving rise to any available remedy under UCL. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Causes of action for conspiracy/aiding and abetting fraudulent conveyance are sufficiently stated =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLOTTE BONIA VS. THE ESTATE OF PRESCOTT ASHE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Due To Plaintiff'S Failure To File Amended Complaint Within Time Allowed By Court
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT THE ESTATE OF PRESCOTT ASHE, NICHOLAS DONOVAN, ASHE VENTURES, LLC, ANNE KENSOK, ROBERT STOBO Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Due To Plaintiff'S Failure To File Amended Complaint Within Time Allowed By Court Hearing Required. The Court will consider Plaintiff's October 26, 2023 and November 2, 2023 filings as a late opposition to the Motion to Vacate. If Plaintiff files and serves before 9:00 a.m. on November 16, 2023 a declaration with a proposed Second Amended Complaint attached (clearly labeled causes of action in clear and concise language asserting facts as to each defendant), the Court will consider allowing the Plaintiff to file such Second Amended Complaint. Otherwise, the Motion to Dismiss will be granted =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL DENNIS VS. NAYEEMA HAI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS WITH DEFENDANTS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 16, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS WITH DEFENDANTS is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005, 1013 and 1010.6. Proof of service filed on November 14, 2023 (2 days before the hearing in violation of CRC 3.1300(c)) indicates electronic service on October 25, 2023, 15 court days before the hearing. The Court notes that the copy of the "Mutual Release in Full of All Claims and Rights" attached to Roderick Brown's declaration is not executed by the defendants =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
